The fact that the checking account balance is negative, because it serves as a robust predictor of default probability, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, and the model also identifies it as a factor that increases risk. [1]
The fact that there is no checking account, because of constrained banking records, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, and the model also identifies it as a factor that increases risk. [2]
The fact that the loan duration is extended, because it prolongs lender exposure to borrower uncertainty, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, but the model instead identifies it as a factor that decreases risk. [3]
Overall, this applicant is likely to have a low risk of default.
